title: model_routing=auto is live but DORMANT: the advisor never emits {cli,tier} for existing phases — elicit + verify end-to-end
description:
EVIDENCE (2026-07-02): model_routing defaults to auto (phase-registry.json, MR4/cycle-440) and the full apply chain is wired (schema MR1 -> clamp MR2 -> projection+soft-overlay MR4 -> degrade path), BUT cycle-446's advisor-response-plan.txt contains ZERO cli/tier fields (grep -c '"(cli|tier)"' = 0; run/skip only) and the runner log shows no overlay applications. The advisor exercises sequencing authority (it skipped scout in 446) but never proposes the model lever, so auto mode is a no-op in practice and every phase still dispatches profile-static models.

ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESES (verify in order): (1) buildPlanPrompt/composePlanPrompt (core/phase_advisor.go) projects guardrails but does not clearly INSTRUCT the advisor to emit per-phase {cli,tier} for EXISTING phases (MintSpec-only habit); (2) the JSON schema/example in the prompt omits the optional fields so the LLM never produces them; (3) parse path drops them silently (less likely — MR1 tests cover parse).

REQUIRED (TDD red-first):
1. PROMPT ELICITATION: the plan prompt must show the {cli,tier} fields in its response schema/example, state WHEN to propose them (e.g. deep-reasoning phases -> tier top/high; mechanical phases -> fast; CLI only when deviating from profile), and keep them optional (absent = profile default; the degrade path stays).
2. GOLDEN REPLAY TEST: extend the routingeval/replay corpus with a plan response carrying {cli,tier} and assert the runner's dispatch line reflects the clamped overlay (source=advisor-overlay) under model_routing=auto; and that advisory mode only LOGS it (routing-decision artifact).
3. OBSERVABILITY: the runner must log applied overlays ('[runner] phase=X advisor overlay cli=Y tier=Z (clamped from ...)') and the cycle dossier should record per-phase model source (profile|pin|advisor) — without this, dormancy is invisible (it took manual artifact archaeology to find).
4. LIVE VERIFICATION: one cycle after the prompt fix, assert >=1 phase in the advisor plan carries a tier AND the dispatch used it (or a clamp event explains why not).

CONSTRAINTS: no relaxation of the clamp (model proposes, kernel disposes); degrade path untouched; tier vocabulary stays abstract (will become top/high/balanced/fast when the tier-vocab slice of latest-model-preference lands — coordinate, do not conflict); apicover -enforce + go test -race clean. Related inbox: latest-model-preference (selection-side; this item is routing-side emission).
